title: Executive Order 9275—Prescribing Additional Regulations Governing Persons and Vessels in Defensive Sea Areas

flags.justification: Exercises specific, existing criminal-code authority for a narrow, defined restriction (camera possession in designated defensive sea areas).
Sec. 4 cross-references the specific Criminal Code provision governing prosecution, so ordinary judicial criminal process applies.
Multiple related provisions (possession ban, custody duty for ship officers, notice-posting requirement) all coherently serve one purpose: photography security in defensive areas.
The prohibition is reasonably specific, with explicit exceptions for official duty and for licensed activity under published regulations (Public Law 627).
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
No formal review or termination mechanism is specified, tied implicitly to the wartime duration of the defensive sea area designations.
Ordinary criminal judicial process applies to any prosecution; no oversight is removed.
Normal criminal enforcement via the courts provides the relevant check.
A narrow, content-neutral, security-specific restriction, bounded in scope despite touching expressive activity, far narrower than detention-based wartime security measures.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.
The official-duty and licensed-activity exceptions are narrow and purpose-consistent, not broad undermining carve-outs.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 1/18 = 5.6%. A wartime security measure touching expressive activity (photography), but narrow, content-neutral, and criminally enforced through ordinary courts rather than through an extrajudicial mechanism.
